key_moments.title: Fencers assume en garde position → Fighters assume ready stance
key_moments.description: Two fencers face each other on the strip, raising their foils and assuming the en garde position at the start of the bout. → Two fighters face each other on the mat, raising their swords and taking the ready stance at the start of the match.
key_moments.tactic: Standard opening stance, assessing distance and opponent readiness → Standard opening stance, judging distance and opponent readiness

key_moments.title: Initial attack and parry → First strike and block
key_moments.description: One fencer launches a thrust attack which is parried by the opponent, who attempts a counter-riposte. → One fighter launches a poke strike which is blocked by the opponent, who tries a counter-return.
key_moments.tactic: Standard attack-defend sequence, testing opponent's reaction time → Standard strike-defend sequence, testing opponent's reaction time

key_moments.title: Defensive parry-riposte → Defensive block-return
key_moments.description: Fencer executes a circular parry sixte followed by a quick riposte to the opponent's flank. → Fighter does a circular block to the upper-right followed by a quick return to the opponent's side.
key_moments.tactic: Defensive parry-riposte combination, a fundamental foil technique → Defensive block-return combination, a basic sword technique

key_moments.title: Score awarded after exchange → Point given after exchange
key_moments.description: After a quick exchange, the referee halts the bout and awards a touch to one fencer, updating the scoreboard. → After a quick exchange, the referee stops the match and gives a point to one fighter, updating the scoreboard.
key_moments.tactic: Quick touch scored via preparation attack → Quick point scored through setup strike

key_moments.title: Aggressive lunge attack → Aggressive forward-thrust strike
key_moments.description: Fencer on the right executes a full lunge attack extending fully into the opponent's guard, scoring a valid touch. → Fighter on the right does a full forward-thrust strike extending fully into the opponent's guard, scoring a valid point.
key_moments.tactic: Aggressive fleche/lunge attack, committing fully to score a touch → Aggressive running/forward-thrust strike, committing fully to score a point

key_moments.title: Fencers reset after intense exchange → Fighters reset after intense exchange
key_moments.description: After a flurry of attacks and parries, both fencers retreat to their starting lines to reset position. → After a burst of strikes and blocks, both fighters go back to their starting lines to reset position.
key_moments.tactic: Resetting distance to reassess and plan next attack → Resetting distance to rethink and plan next strike

key_moments.title: Counter-attack on preparation → Counter-strike on setup
key_moments.description: Fencer executes a perfectly timed counter-attack as the opponent begins their advance. → Fighter does a perfectly timed counter-strike as the opponent starts their advance.
key_moments.tactic: Counter-time tactic, catching opponent in preparation phase → Counter-time tactic, catching opponent in setup phase

key_moments.title: Tactical touch in second period → Tactical point in second period
key_moments.description: A carefully executed second-intention attack earns a touch after drawing the opponent's parry. → A carefully done trick-strike earns a point after drawing out the opponent's block.
key_moments.tactic: Second intention attack, a classic high-level foil tactic → Trick-strike, a classic high-level sword tactic

key_moments.title: Strong defensive</think>
key_moments.description: Fencer successfully defends a strong attack and recovers to en garde without yielding ground. → Fighter successfully guards against a strong strike and recovers to ready stance without losing ground.
key_moments.tactic: Defensive priority management, maintaining right-of-way → Defensive priority management, keeping priority

key_moments.title: Final bout conclusion → Final match end
key_moments.description: The bout concludes with a handshake between the fencers as the referee announces the final score. → The match ends with a handshake between the fighters as the referee announces the final score.
key_moments.tactic: Sportsmanship ritual at the conclusion of competition → Sportsmanship ritual at the end of competition

actions.note: Both fencers raise weapons and assume standard en garde position

actions.action: Both fencers raise weapons and assume standard en garde position → Both fighters raise weapons and take standard ready stance
actions.note: Fencers close distance in measured steps
Fencer on left extends arm in a direct thrust

actions.action: Fencers close distance in measured steps → Fighters close distance in measured steps
actions.note: Circular parry followed by immediate counter-attack

actions.action: Fencer on left extends arm in a direct thrust → Fighter on left extends arm in a direct poke
Circular parry followed by immediate counter-attack → Circular block followed by immediate counter-strike

actions.note: Strong lateral parry deflecting opponent's blade

actions.action: Full extension lunge attack → Full extension forward thrust strike
actions.note: Traditional handshake between competitors at bout end

text_in_video: STRATIS ARENA → STRATIS ARENA (keep as name)
SALE MARIE → SALE MARIE (keep as name)
STRATIS ARENA BASKETBALL → STRATIS ARENA BASKETBALL (keep as name)
summary: The video captures a fencing competition held at the Stratis Arena, featuring multiple strips where athletes compete simultaneously. The camera focuses on the central strip where two foil fencers engage in a complete bout. The match showcases fundamental and advanced techniques including en garde positioning, measured advances, thrust attacks, parry-riposte combinations, full lunge attacks, and counter-attacks. The right fencer, distinguished by blue socks, demonstrates aggressive attacking while the left fencer employs tactical defense and counter-time strategies. The bout progresses through multiple exchanges with touches awarded based on right-of-way rules. The video concludes with the traditional handshake between competitors, exemplifying the sportsmanship inherent in fencing.
